plan_adherence_notes: All 4 plan steps were executed. Step 1 (GET tests): 6/7 planned tests implemented — explicit nextFromSeq-is-null assertion is absent as a standalone test but pagination tests implicitly exercise this. Step 2 (POST validation tests): all 6 planned tests plus a bonus (empty idempotency key). Step 3 (frontend tests): all 8 planned test scenarios implemented. Step 4 (full suite run): backend tests pass (15/15); frontend tests could not be verified due to missing node_modules in worktree (infrastructure, not a code issue). The plan's child decomposition (backend child-1, frontend child-2) was executed correctly with parallel branches merged back.
missed_questions: Should delete/edit operations be supported for comments? (not in task spec but common for comment systems)
What are the rate limiting thresholds for comment posting? (existing rate limiter was not tested in new tests)
Should the frontend tests use real React Query integration or mock the API layer? (decision was made implicitly — chose API mocking over hook mocking, which is better)
reason: The plan correctly identified the remaining work (test coverage gaps) after research revealed the feature was already fully implemented. Execution added 14 new backend tests covering GET pagination, POST validation, and idempotency, plus 8 frontend component tests — all aligned with the plan. Backend tests pass (15/15 including the pre-existing POST contract test). Minor gaps: no explicit nextFromSeq boundary test and frontend tests could not be run in this worktree due to missing node_modules. Overall the implementation is thorough, well-structured, and faithful to the plan.
